https://www.wanted.co.kr/wd/365244.description:
**Recruitment of Planning Interns**
**(Important) Refer to the link for details:** https://works.choi.build/planning-intern-recruitment

We are looking for someone who can grow into a planner, rather than someone with a lot of planning experience. This is not a position for someone who is already skilled from the start. Instead, we want someone who is willing to go through the process of reading, thinking, revising, and recreating. Planning is not just about coming up with ideas, but about making judgments and proving them with results.

**Selection of Planning Interns:** 5 people
**On-the-job training:** Hands-on training and tasks to learn and grow
**Final conversion:** 3 people

This internship is not an easy one. We set clear standards from the beginning. This is not a casual internship where you can just gain experience. It's a process where you'll work on real tasks, revise, and recreate to learn the basics of planning in a short period.

**What we look for in a planner:**
1. Planning is not about intuition, but about solving problems.
2. Planning is not a job that only requires one skill.
3. We hire people who are not yet complete, and that's intentional.
4. You'll work with our representative planner to learn the standards.
5. We'll openly share what kind of person we're looking for and what kind of person is not a good fit.
6. We'll share our selection process and training content.
7. We'll share the tasks you'll be working on and the qualifications required.
8. Our application form asks about your actual experiences, not just what you say.

**What planning means to us:**
Planning is not just about having a good sense or taste. A good planner combines knowledge, structure, data, aesthetics, market sense, and execution power. From the outside, it may seem like the following people are all similar:

* Someone who speaks well
* Someone with many ideas
* Someone who knows a lot about trends
* Someone who can make documents look good
* Someone with a good sense of style

But a real planner is different. A real planner is not just someone who can speak well, but someone who can organize complex problems, read users and markets, and turn them into executable answers.

**Planning is not a one-trick job:**
Platform, brand, content, data, advertising, and AI are not separate tasks. To move a business forward, you need to connect them. Users see the brand, react to content, behave within products, leave data trails, and come back through ads and social media. A planner reads this flow, judges what needs to be fixed, and turns it into an executable plan. That's why we're looking for someone who can learn and connect multiple fields quickly, rather than someone who is only good at one thing.

**Why we hire incomplete people:**
What's more important than experience is the ability to learn and improve. We want to find and nurture people who have the foundation to grow into planners. Even among experienced planners, there are many who have grown in the wrong way. We don't want those kinds of planners. They may have ideas, but lack execution plans, make documents with unclear cores, talk about brands and UX but don't really understand users and markets, use AI and tools but can't make their own judgments, and participate in meetings but can't deliver results.

**What's important to us:**
* Someone who reads a lot
* Someone who learns quickly
* Someone who accepts feedback
* Someone who doesn't give up easily on difficult problems
* Someone who acknowledges and improves their weaknesses quickly
* Someone who shows results rather than just talking

**Working with our representative planner:**
This is an opportunity to learn the planner's thought process and work standards through real tasks.

**What I've worked on:**
I've worked on digital campaigns and performance marketing for Hyundai Card, direct digital marketing for Samsung Insurance, and overseas marketing for the city of Seoul. I've written and presented proposals for major pitches, and I've come up with the city's brand slogan "Seoul, My Soul" in 2012. My pitch win rate is around 95%.

**What you'll learn:**
This is not just bragging. The results I've achieved are not just based on intuition, but on combining knowledge, theory, market understanding, market sense, data, and execution plans. As an intern, you'll learn how ideas are turned into market, user, brand, product, document, and execution plans.

**What we're looking for in a person:**
We care more about what you've actually done than what you say you'll do in the future. Results and feedback are more important than words and intuition.

A. Someone who can turn what they've read into their own thoughts
You should be able to read books, find materials, learn new concepts, and summarize difficult content in your own words. We'll look at what you've read, what you've spent your time on, and what you've actually achieved.

B. Someone who can see phenomena as structures
A good planner doesn't just look at phenomena as functions or feelings. They see how users flow in, what they do, and where they leave. We'll look at what users see, what actions they take, and where they're satisfied or leave.

C. Someone with aesthetics and market sense
It's not enough to just say something is "pretty" or "not pretty". You should be able to explain why something looks sophisticated. You should be able to see why the public likes something, but the industry doesn't, and vice versa. You should be able to separate your own taste from the public's reaction.

D. Someone who can connect new tools to results
You shouldn't be afraid of new AI, document tools, design tools, data tools, or collaboration tools. We'll look at what you've made with these tools, not just what you know. Can you touch and try new tools?
We want to reduce repetitive tasks and become faster at doing the same task the second time around. 
We like people who have their own opinions. However, it's difficult to work with those who cannot adjust their thoughts even after receiving feedback.
One must acknowledge what they don't know.
Feedback should not be used as an excuse.
Personal growth should take priority over saving face.
We look for people who improve through feedback and practice. 
We want someone who can complete tasks from start to finish, and whose work improves after receiving feedback. 
This person should be able to accumulate skills over time.
They should not give up easily on difficult problems and be diligent in their studies.
Their words and actions should be consistent.

We don't fit with people who:
- only come up with ideas but don't want to execute and follow through
- explain a lot but lack actual results
- take feedback personally and defensively
- make judgments based on intuition without studying enough
- mistake their personal taste for public sense
- only follow the surface-level format without understanding the underlying principles
- rely on memory instead of keeping records
- repeatedly receive the same feedback
- can't work collaboratively and only focus on individual progress
- get easily swayed under pressure
- change their words and actions depending on the situation
- are careless with company time, information, and promises

Instead of just saying "I want to do it," we look at what you've already done.
We can't judge based on statements like "I'll work hard," "I want to grow," or "I want to succeed."
We look at the evidence of time you've already invested.
- What have you read?
- What have you created?
- What have you repeated?
- What have you given up on?
- What feedback have you received and how have you changed?
- What tools have you actually used?
- What competitions have you participated in and ranked high in?
- How long have you persisted?
Many people want to succeed, but few have actually read, created, improved, and learned from their failures.

Our selection process and training content:
We first select 5 candidates, and then work on actual tasks and projects together. 
We evaluate their potential to grow as planners in 6 areas.
After the training, we will choose the final 3 candidates to join our team.
We disclose this information from the start because this is not just a simple internship, but a process to find someone to work with.
The evaluation order is: feedback response, ability to complete tasks, learning speed, reliability, and potential to grow as a planner.

The 6 areas of evaluation are:
A. Basic work skills: setting up laptops, tools, and accounts, managing to-do lists and calendars, reporting, asking questions, and receiving feedback, reading and summarizing routines.
B. AI practical setup: setting up AI environments, cloud and codec settings, harness, skill, and MCP, drafting, revising, and reviewing methods.
C. App understanding: understanding the overall structure of the app, user flow, screen and function intentions, and setting usability standards.
D. Figma execution: Figma basics, writing wireframes, organizing screen flows, and development delivery structures.
E. Problem-solving: finding problems, identifying causes and solution options, execution plans, and verification metrics, field-specific thinking frameworks.
F. Marketing basics: targets, messages, materials, media, budgets, conversions, sign-ups, activations, and retention, growth improvement ideas.

The tasks include:
- Cre8orClub service and UX planning assistance
- Evaluating app usability and summarizing improvement plans
- Research on brands, exhibitions, content, and platforms
- AI and tool utilization research and work automation experiments
- Organizing user feedback
- Organizing planning documents, meeting minutes, and to-do tasks
- Competitive service analysis
- Structuring content and campaign ideas
- Summarizing improvement plans based on data and user behavior

Working hours:
- Business hours: 9:30 to 18:30
- Annual leave, half leave, and flexible leave can be used without reason

Work environment:
- In-house lounge and snack bar (Snack24 service)
- Americano machine (coffee beans) and unlimited support for various types of tea
- No dress code

Support for work:
- Company location: 3-minute walk from Chungmuro Station, Line 4
- Holiday homecoming expenses paid
- Snacks provided (separate snack bar and coffee machine operated)
- Transportation expenses provided (taxi fares for work, etc.)

https://www.wanted.co.kr/wd/365002.description:
Beyond Possibility, Creating Innovation into Reality, Deogrose.

Deogrose is a startup that creates new values beyond limits and grows into a global K-beauty brand. 

With a young team led by management in their 20s who graduated from Seoul National University, 
we launched the 'glow.m' (beauty device & cosmetics) brand in 2023 and achieved monthly sales of 10 billion won in just one month and annual sales of 30 billion won in one year.

By 2025, just two years after its launch, we achieved 10 billion won in sales, maintaining an explosive growth rate that is rare in the industry.

Deogrose is not only successful in the domestic market but also in Japan and the US, continuing to play and succeed in these markets.

--
[Deogrose Culture] - Check your fit with Deogrose before applying!
1. We feel dopamine in growth and high performance
• This is a place where people who create confidence and happiness, which the world needs, and produce outstanding performance, are more important than those who pursue job security by performing moderate tasks and achieving moderate goals.
• It's a place where you can solve unsolvable problems and difficult challenges with great colleagues and spend your prime working life.
• If you're not someone who feels dopamine in growth, you might not enjoy life at Deogrose.

1-1. Self Check!
• Do you feel more dopamine in solving difficult challenges and achieving them than in performing moderate tasks and pursuing job security?
• Do you want to spend your prime working life in an environment where you solve high-level challenges with great colleagues, rather than in a stable and predictable environment?
• Do you feel anxious if you don't feel like you're improving every day? Is that feeling the most important criterion for choosing a job?

2. We are a professional sports team made up of excellent individuals
• An excellent individual is someone with exceptional abilities and a high sense of responsibility.
• We define exceptional abilities as the ability to hypothesize, verify, and creatively solve problems in a self-directed manner.
• We motivate and discipline ourselves, and we overcome and improve ourselves.
• We don't wait for instructions, and we don't think "that's not my job."

2-1. Self Check!
• When faced with an unsolved problem, do you naturally approach it by hypothesizing and verifying?
• Do you tend to follow organizational rules, such as working hours, even when no one is checking?
• Is it a habit for you to learn and train yourself to do better, like a professional athlete trains outside of games?
• Do you stop when you feel like you've reached your limit, or do you encourage yourself to go beyond it?
• When was the last time you thought "that's not my job"? How did you actually behave in that situation?

3. We don't use DMs
• We believe that transparently shared information is important, so it's our principle to make all information public.
• We share as much information as possible so that each person can make the best judgment and execution in their position.
• Anyone can see the current focus, decision-making background, and experimental results.
• By sharing context, we can make judgments that consider the entire company.

3-1. Self Check!
• Is it natural for you to share the context, decision-making background, and experimental results of your work with the entire organization?
• When communicating with colleagues, do you prefer to use public channels rather than DMs?
• If you think the context you know could help your colleagues' judgment, do you share it without being asked?

4. We need to earn trust from our colleagues to work smoothly
• We create high performance through trust among colleagues.
• Trust starts with playing our roles and producing good results.
• By demonstrating excellence in our positions and solving problems proactively, we make our colleagues trust our judgment and execution.

4-1. Self Check!
• Have you ever had an experience where a colleague trusted your judgment and executed it without double-checking? Where did that trust come from?
• Have you ever had an experience where the lack of trust hindered the progress of work? How did you build trust in that situation?
• Do you agree that producing excellent results in your area is the most reliable way to work smoothly with colleagues?

5. We are a place where people who are not afraid of feedback grow
• Feedback is the only tool for growth.
• On the basis of trust, we give and receive feedback boldly with each other.
• We speak honestly and listen willingly for the growth of our colleagues and Deogrose.
• We are people who prioritize giving and receiving feedback over maintaining comfort.

5-1. Self Check!
• When you see room for improvement in a colleague's work, do you speak up directly or wait for them to notice?
• When you receive feedback, is your initial reaction defensive or curious?
• Do you believe that giving and receiving honest feedback strengthens relationships rather than damaging them?

6. We believe in rules and ways to win
• We understand that our work code and rules exist to produce results.
• And we are a place where people who practice these methods with high responsibility gather.

6-1. Self Check!
• Do you believe that the way an organization works exists to produce results, not to restrict individuals?
• Is it more natural for you to follow the team's rules with high responsibility rather than doing things your own way?
• Do you feel more engaged when working within clear rules, or do you feel constrained?

* This is a 3-month convertible intern position.
* This position is scheduled to be considered for conversion to full-time position / contract extension / contract termination after a 3-month contract.

[Introducing the team we will be joining]

Channel Talk's sales organization is a team that creates business growth using 'aggressive sales' as a weapon. This recruitment will be conducted simultaneously in two core teams at both ends of the sales cycle.

The IS (Inside Sales) team is the starting point of Channel Talk sales. We perform both inbound and outbound in a non-face-to-face environment, but we are a team that is particularly strong in outbound, with cold calling as our main weapon. We go beyond simply creating meeting spaces, and create high-quality sales opportunities that can lead to actual sales.

The CSM (Customer Success Manager) team is a partner responsible for the long-term success of Channel Talk's major customers. This is a team that discovers complex business problems in the field closest to customers and solves them through product utilization strategies and business consulting. Among them, the role we are recruiting for this time is to lead existing clients to adopt Channel Talk's AI product (ALF) and lead to actual results.

Both teams are practicing ‘aggressive sales’ by identifying real problems through conversations with customers and proposing solutions through channel talks. There is an open career path where you can use this career as a stepping stone to grow in the direction of Channel Talk leadership. After joining the company, the position will be assigned to either the IS team or the CSM team.

[The charm of Channel Talk at a glance! [Why our team is special]
Website: https://channel.io/ko
Blog: https://channel.io/ko/blog
YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCy4L-mlTgH-v8vBbtzCGhIA
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/channel.io/
IS team job interview: https://channel.io/ko/team/blog/articles/20346983
CSM team job interview: https://channel.io/ko/team/blog/articles/80e029f8

[What opportunities are there?] 
• At the forefront of AI sales – Gain experience at the center of AI transformation at a time when it is the hottest.
• OK even without experience – there are no restrictions on education, major, or experience.
• Opportunity to transition to full-time position – In fact, many of our leaders are former interns.
• High-speed growth environment – ​​Opportunity to grow your sales capabilities through hands-on experience.
• Industry-leading compensation – 3 million won per month.

[What kind of work do you do?]
• IS (Inside Sales) Team
• Directly list companies that can adopt Channel Talk and create a database.
• Using cold calling as our main strategy, we quickly identify the needs of new customers and select sales targets through intensive communication such as follow-up calls and follow-up emails.
• Identify clear needs and think about and provide ways to solve problems through channel talk.
• Analyze the funnel for each potential customer inflow path and consider the process for higher conversion.
• Proactively check and follow up on the progress of the sales process to achieve weekly and monthly goals.
 
• Customer Success Manager (CSM) Team
• We sell Channel Talk's AI product (ALF) to customers who generate the largest number of sales in the country and Channel Talk.
• We identify customer needs and suggest appropriate consulting to ensure continuous use of Channel Talk.
• Identify customer companies that are likely to adopt ALF and discover their needs directly through calls.
• Together we will advance the sales script that reflects ALF’s sales direction.
• Understand the client's business situation and suggest ways in which ALF can solve real-world problems.
• Proactively tracks and achieves weekly and monthly upsell goals.

• The channel team strives to provide the best treatment in the industry to members who have achieved meaningful results.
• We provide meaningful stock options to those who have made significant contributions over the long term.
• Provides an environment where you can focus and become more immersed in your work.

| Introducing the planning and marketing team
The Iris Bright Planning and Marketing Team is the team that creates the first point of contact between the brand and consumers. We analyze product strengths and consumer needs to discover persuasive messages and deliver them in the most effective format and channel to plan content that increases purchase conversion and brand trust.

We are involved in the entire brand journey from product launch to growth stage, and design differentiated campaigns and content strategies by sensitively responding to market changes and trends. One idea leads the entire process from planning/production/performance/improvement, and we aim to be the first to discover and best convey the brand's appeal.

1. Derive appeal points and develop message strategy
· Discover the strengths of the product from the consumer's perspective and structure it into a persuasive message

• An excellent individual is someone with exceptional abilities and a high sense of responsibility.
• We define exceptional abilities as the ability to hypothesize, verify, and creatively solve problems in a self-directed manner.
• We motivate and discipline ourselves, and improve and overcome ourselves.
• We don't wait for instructions, and we don't think "that's not my job."

• When faced with an unsolved problem, is it natural for you to approach it by hypothesizing and verifying?
• Are you the type of person who keeps organizational rules, such as working hours, even when no one is checking?
• Is it a habit for you to learn and train yourself to improve, like a professional athlete, even outside of work?
• Do you stop when you feel like you've reached your limit, or do you push yourself to go beyond?
• When was the last time you thought "that's not my job"? How did you actually behave in that situation?

3. We don't use DMs
• We believe that transparently shared information is important, so it's our principle to make all information public.
• We share as much information as possible so that each person can make the best judgment and execution in their position.
• Anyone can see the current focus area, the background of decision-making, and the results of experiments.
• By sharing context, we can make judgments that consider the entire company.

3-1. Self Check!
• Is it natural for you to publicly share the context, decision-making background, and experimental results of the work you're doing?
• When communicating with colleagues, do you prefer to use public channels rather than DMs?
• If you think the context you know could help your colleagues' judgment, do you share it without being asked?

4. We need to earn trust from our colleagues to work smoothly
• We create high performance through trust among colleagues.
• Trust starts with producing good results by fulfilling one's role.
• By proving excellence in one's position and solving tasks proactively, we make our colleagues trust our judgment and execution.

4-1. Self Check!
• Have you ever had an experience where a colleague trusted your judgment and executed it without double-checking? Where did that trust come from?
• Have you ever had an experience where a lack of trust hindered the progress of work? How did you build trust in that situation?
• Do you agree that producing excellent results in your area is the most reliable way to work smoothly with colleagues?

5. This is a place where people who are not afraid of feedback grow

• Do you believe that giving and receiving honest feedback strengthens relationships rather than damaging them?

6. We believe in rules and how to win
